2603.25112.title: Do LLMs Know What They Know? Measuring Metacognitive Efficiency with Signal Detection Theory
2603.25112.paper_summary: Standard evaluation of LLM confidence relies on calibration metrics (ECE, Brier score) that conflate two distinct capacities: how much a model knows (Type-1 sensitivity) and how well it knows what it knows (Type-2 metacognitive sensitivity). We introduce an evaluation framework based on Type-2 Signal Detection Theory that decomposes these capacities using meta-d' and the metacognitive efficiency ratio M-ratio. Applied to four LLMs (Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3, Llama-3-8B-Base, Gemma-2-9B-Instruct) across 224,000 factual QA trials, we find: (1) metacognitive efficiency varies substantially across models even when Type-1 sensitivity is similar -- Mistral achieves the highest d' but the lowest M-ratio; (2) metacognitive efficiency is domain-specific, with different models showing different weakest domains, invisible to aggregate metrics; (3) temperature manipulation shifts Type-2 criterion while meta-d' remains stable for two of four models, dissociating confidence policy from metacognitive capacity; (4) AUROC_2 and M-ratio produce fully inverted model rankings, demonstrating these metrics answer fundamentally different evaluation questions. The meta-d' framework reveals which models "know what they don't know" versus which merely appear well-calibrated due to criterion placement -- a distinction with direct implications for model selection, deployment, and human-AI collaboration. Pre-registered analysis; code and data publicly available.
2603.25112.paper_summary_zh: 標準的 LLM 信心評估依賴於校準指標（ECE、Brier 分數），這些指標混合了兩種不同的能力：模型知道多少（Type-1 敏感度）以及它對自己所知的了解程度（Type-2 元認知敏感度）。我們引入了一個基於 Type-2 信號檢測理論的評估框架，利用 meta-d' 和元認知效率比率 M-ratio 來分解這些能力。應用於四個 LLM（Llama-3-8B-Instruct、Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3、Llama-3-8B-Base、Gemma-2-9B-Instruct）在 224,000 次事實問答試驗中，我們發現：（1）即使 Type-1 敏感度相似，元認知效率在模型之間仍有顯著差異——Mistral 在 d' 上達到最高但 M-ratio 最低；（2）元認知效率是領域特定的，不同模型顯示出不同的最弱領域，這在綜合指標中是不可見的；（3）溫度操控會改變 Type-2 標準，而對於四個模型中的兩個，meta-d' 保持穩定，將信心政策與元認知能力區分開來；（4）AUROC_2 和 M-ratio 產生完全相反的模型排名，顯示這些指標回答的評估問題根本不同。meta-d' 框架揭示哪些模型「知道自己不知道什麼」，與哪些模型僅因標準位置而看似良好校準——這一區別對模型選擇、部署和人類-AI 協作具有直接影響。預註冊分析；代碼和數據公開可用。
2603.25112.author: Jon-Paul Cacioli et.al.
